findings.title: Privilege escalation: authenticated user self-elevates to admin via PostgREST INSERT on profiles
findings.assumption_violated: GRANT INSERT ON profiles TO authenticated is table-level; the migration comment asserts 'only service_role can write is_admin' but that claim only holds for UPDATE, not INSERT
evidence: Migration 20260417100200 issued REVOKE ALL then GRANT INSERT ON profiles TO authenticated at table level (not column-scoped) because 'Postgres column-scoped INSERT grants don't cover the common insert with defaults path'
Migration 20260502110000 adds the is_admin column with DEFAULT false but no CHECK constraint enforcing is_admin = false
The INSERT RLS policy 'Users can insert their own profile' only checks: WITH CHECK (app.current_user_id() = id) — it does not restrict column values
Attack: a user whose profile row does not exist (e.g. handle_new_user trigger failed during signup) POSTs to /rest/v1/profiles with body {"id": "<their_uuid>", "is_admin": true}
PostgreSQL evaluates: table-level INSERT grant (passes), column DEFAULT overridden by explicit value (passes), RLS WITH CHECK (id matches, passes)
Result: profiles row with is_admin=true is committed
On next request, hooks.server.ts line 54-57 queries profiles.is_admin for the authenticated user and returns true
event.locals.isAdmin = true -> feedback gate bypass at line 96, admin layout passes at admin/+layout.server.ts line 9
The trigger failure precondition is not exotic: any Supabase network timeout during signup, or a future code path that creates auth.users without triggering handle_new_user, creates this opening
findings.fix: Replace table-level GRANT INSERT with column-scoped INSERT grants that exclude is_admin, OR add a BEFORE INSERT OR UPDATE trigger that enforces NEW.is_admin = false (raising an exception otherwise), OR add a CHECK constraint: CHECK (is_admin = false) with only service_role allowed to bypass via the trigger-set default. The cleanest fix is a trigger: CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION enforce_is_admin_immutable() RETURNS trigger AS $$ BEGIN IF NEW.is_admin = true AND (SELECT auth.uid()) IS NOT NULL THEN RAISE EXCEPTION 'is_admin cannot be set by authenticated users'; END IF; RETURN NEW; END; $$ LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER.

findings.title: Split-brain admin authority: new admins bypass the feedback gate but see empty data in admin panel
findings.description: The app layer (hooks.server.ts) now uses profiles.is_admin as the admin authority. The DB layer RLS policies on contacts, invitations, and feedback tables still use auth.jwt() -> 'app_metadata' ->> 'role' = 'admin' (set in migration 20260408_admin_panel.sql, never superseded). Any admin granted via profiles.is_admin=true without the corresponding app_metadata entry has app-layer privileges but no DB-layer data access.
evidence: Migration 20260408_admin_panel.sql created RLS policies: 'Admins can view contacts' (contacts FOR SELECT), 'Admins can create invitations' (invitations FOR INSERT), 'Admins can read invitations' (invitations FOR SELECT), 'Admins read all feedback' (feedback FOR SELECT) — all using USING ((SELECT auth.jwt() -> 'app_metadata' ->> 'role') = 'admin')
Migration 20260502110000 adds profiles.is_admin and backfills from app_metadata, but does NOT update these four RLS policies
No subsequent migration updates the 20260408 policies to reference is_admin
The admin panel pages (waitlist, feedback) use locals.supabase (anon-key, RLS enforced), not a service-role client
A new admin granted by setting profiles.is_admin=true: event.locals.isAdmin=true (line 57 hooks.server.ts) -> admin layout check passes (admin/+layout.server.ts:9) -> admin panel opens
BUT locals.supabase.from('contacts').select(...) -> RLS check: app_metadata role='admin'? No. Returns empty array.
Same for invitations and feedback: all return empty. Admin UI silently shows no data.
The feedback gate bypass at hooks.server.ts:96 DOES work correctly because it uses event.locals.isAdmin, not the DB query
The split only manifests when the new admin tries to use admin panel features that require data from the protected tables
Existing admins who were backfilled from app_metadata in 20260502110000 are unaffected — they have both is_admin=true AND app_metadata.role='admin'
findings.fix: Add a follow-on migration that replaces the four app_metadata RLS policies with is_admin equivalents. The pattern: USING (EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM profiles WHERE profiles.id = app.current_user_id() AND profiles.is_admin = true)). Or, if performance matters, use a SECURITY DEFINER function is_current_user_admin() that does the profile lookup once.

findings.title: Cascade: Supabase substrate unavailable during logout leaves active session and unhandled 500
findings.description: Both logout paths call identityPort.invalidate({} as Session) without try/catch. The adapter's invalidate calls supabase.auth.signOut(). If Supabase is unreachable, the adapter throws SubstrateUnavailableError (an unhandled exception). SvelteKit converts this to a 500 response. The redirect to /login or / never executes. The user's session cookie is not cleared. On the next request, safeGetSession() finds the still-valid cookie, revalidates it against Supabase (now possibly back online), and the user is still authenticated.
evidence: adapter.ts line 82-84: invalidate calls supabase.auth.signOut() with no error handling — on network failure this propagates as SubstrateUnavailableError (thrown at the supabase client level)
logout/+page.server.ts line 14: await locals.identityPort.invalidate({} as Session) — no try/catch wrapping
login/+page.server.ts line 47: same pattern for the logout form action
SvelteKit's unhandled exception in a server action returns a 500 error page
The redirect(303, '/') on line 16 of logout/+page.server.ts only executes if invalidate() resolves without throwing
The Supabase SSR client sets cookies on the response via the setAll callback only when signOut() completes; a thrown exception before cookie clearance means the auth cookie remains
On Cloudflare Pages edge, a transient Supabase outage during a logout attempt permanently locks out the user from being able to log out until the substrate recovers
If the user closes the browser tab after seeing the 500, their server-side session remains active until natural token expiry
findings.fix: Wrap invalidate() in try/catch in both logout handlers and proceed with the redirect regardless: try { await locals.identityPort.invalidate({} as Session); } catch { /* best-effort; cookie may persist until expiry */ } redirect(303, '/'). Alternatively, clear the Supabase auth cookies directly in the catch block as a fallback.

residual_risks.description: The is_admin column is selectable by any authenticated user for any profile row. Any logged-in user can enumerate the full admin roster via GET /rest/v1/profiles?select=id,username,is_admin&is_admin=eq.true. The migration comment acknowledges this as 'not sensitive' but the admin roster is operationally sensitive information (targeted social engineering, knowing who to impersonate).

resetPassword action at login/+page.server.ts line 64 returns error.message from Supabase directly: return fail(400, { email: email.toString(), error: error.message }). This violates the CLAUDE.md standard 'Never return internal error details' and the Supabase error for a non-existent email may differ from a known-email error, enabling account enumeration. This is a single-boundary issue owned by reliability-reviewer but noted because the same login route now routes auth through the identity port for the login action but bypasses it for resetPassword.

testing_gaps.description: No integration test covers the INSERT privilege escalation path: authenticated user with no profile row attempting to INSERT a profile with is_admin=true via PostgREST.

No test covers the admin split-brain: a user with is_admin=true but no app_metadata.role='admin' should see empty contacts/invitations/feedback in the admin panel, but this failure is currently silent.
No test covers SubstrateUnavailableError propagating from invalidate() — the logout failure cascade is not exercised.
